summary: A kind declares its nesting template kind-side — the child kind plus, for file children, the path pattern relative to the parent's unit — and an admission over the host overrides the child kind

new.description: Regenerated seam binding for the new Rust `TemplateRow` (ts-rs output — regenerate with `BLESS_SEAM_BINDINGS=1`, never hand-edit).

edit.description: `KindFacts` (102) carries no template fact at all — a kind's templating is inferred corpus-side from admissions, so it reaches only embedded children. Add the declared fact representation.md names: per inner layer, the child kind plus the path pattern relative to the parent's unit when children are files. Riders: the pre-0019 fact-3 "layout" narration (4/16/109/111) and the "posture 3" cite (257) ride this entry.

`templatesFor` (179) derives a host's templates from `AdmissionsByHost` alone. Fold the kind's own declared templates in: the kind-side declaration is the default; an admission over the host overrides its child kind (builtins.md, "The shipped kinds"). An embedded host declares no template, so admission stays its sole source — unchanged.
Regenerated seam binding for the grown `templates` column (ts-rs output).
Re-exports the new `TemplateRow` binding (generated header — regenerate, never hand-edit).

`KindFactRow::templates` (2204) is `Vec<String>` — bare child-kind names. Grow it onto a new ts-rs-derived `TemplateRow` (the presence-coupled sibling `CollectionAddressRow`, 2228, is the shape precedent), and move the writer (2916) and the `from_table` reader (2943) onto it.

`Template` (565) carries only `kind: String`, and its doc scopes a template to the child kind a fence info string names — narrower than representation.md. Grow it with the child's path pattern, and widen `CustomKind::templates` (92), `from_table` (635) and `overlay_templates` (675) onto the grown column.
The freshness gate's type list (`fresh_bindings`, 55) gains `TemplateRow`, and the committed `KindFactRow` binding moves with the regenerated export.
Nesting coverage extends to a declared file-child template: the host kind's row carries the child kind plus its path pattern, read back off the lock.

The template-row assertions (207, 445-455, 477) move onto the grown row shape, and gain the kind-side pair: a custom kind's declared file-child template reaches its row with no admission, and an admission over that host overrides the child kind.

schemaDelta: `KindFactRow.templates` grows from `Array<string>` (bare child-kind names) to one `TemplateRow` per template — child kind plus, for file children, the path pattern relative to the parent's unit. No built-in declares a template, so every built-in row still omits the column and `src/builtin_lock.toml` stays byte-identical.

tests.asserts: A host kind declaring a file-child template round-trips it off the lock — child kind plus path pattern — through `from_table`/`overlay_templates`.
A kind's declared template reaches its fact row with no admission present; an admission over the same host overrides the template's child kind.

acceptance: A kind's nesting template is a declared kind-side fact — the child kind, plus the path pattern relative to the parent's unit when children are files (representation.md, "kind") — reaching the lock through the grown `templates` column, with a corpus admission overriding the child kind (builtins.md, "The shipped kinds"). Templating stops being inferable only from corpus-side admission over an embedded body.
notes: Was SKILL-NESTED-REFERENCE-DOCS; cc5a9b3's ruling (0025 amended) split it — the declared fact is derivable now, the built-in adoption is not (fork `(nested-file-child)`). Scope is the declared fact only — nothing resolves a file child's discovery off the pattern yet, exactly as a host's embedded members already resolve independently of its templates. Do NOT route a file child through `admit`: admission is over an embedded body and refuses a non-embedded kind. scoped at cc5a9b3.

summary: An embedded format places a closed, engine-derived set of facts about its edge targets — name, address, kind, relative projection path — so a rendered reference is true by construction

edit.description: `ResolvedEmbeddedMemberValue` (300) carries kind/key/leaves/collections only — no target facts. Add the closed derived set, keyed by the declaring kind's edge fields (the `EdgeField` interface, 43; `KindFacts.edgeFields`, 126): name, address, kind, and the target's projection path relative to the host member's own projection. Data the template selects, never authored at the instance.

`resolveMemberLeaves` (104) / `renderMemberBlock` (158) derive the target facts from the value's edge-field leaves resolved against the harness's member table — the resolution the mention scope already carries — before the resolved value reaches the `render` hook (159). A dangling target refuses here, the way an unresolvable mention does (pipeline.md, "Emit": Refusing).

A `render` hook placing a target's address and projection path renders it true by construction. Rider: the retired-`renderMemberFence` cite at 937 rides this entry — the one queued entry reconciling this file's comments after TEMPLATE-FILE-CHILD-FACT.
An edge field whose target the program does not resolve refuses at emit.

schemaDelta: none — the target facts are derived at emit and consumed by the erased `render` hook; the engine sees only the rendered string it already saw.

tests.asserts: An embedded kind's `render` hook places an edge target's name/address/kind/relative projection path from the derived facts, never an authored string; the rendered reference matches the resolved target.
An edge field whose target the program does not resolve refuses before a byte is written.

acceptance: An embedded format selects its edge targets' facts as data — a rendered reference is true by construction, and instance prose never spells its target (representation.md, "kind"). The `display: "src/main.js"` restatement class becomes unspellable.
notes: 0025 bullet 2. The closed set is exactly the four facts representation.md names — never fabricated, never widened by build: a fifth fact is a spec question, not a convenience. Serialized behind TEMPLATE-FILE-CHILD-FACT (shared `sdk/src/kind.ts` and `sdk/test/emit.test.ts`). scoped at cc5a9b3.

summary: The format-omits-edge check: a predicate over a member's declared edges asserting its format placed each one, so a format cannot render a contract the prose does not represent

edit.description: Add the predicate variant to the closed `Predicate` enum (81) — the placement check over an edge selection, evaluated at `each` grain by incidence (contract.md, "selection"). Severity is the clause author's, never the tool's. Rider: the retired-`effective` comment at 475 rides this entry.
Evaluate the new predicate and admit it in the admissibility walk (`admissibility`, 97; the vacuous/unfillable bar, 114+).
The clause constructor for the new predicate, with its guidance channel.

Lower the format's placement observation into a declaration row at emit — which of a value's declared edge fields the render hook actually selected from the derived target facts (EMBEDDED-FORMAT-TARGET-FACTS is the observation point). The engine never sees the format and never reads a projection back, so this fact reaches the gate as a declaration row or not at all.
Regenerated seam binding for the grown predicate vocabulary (ts-rs output).
Predicate coverage for the placement check — held and violated.

schemaDelta: The closed predicate vocabulary grows by one variant (a deliberate language change, ratified by 0025); a declaration row family carries emit's per-value record of which declared edge fields the format placed.

tests.asserts: A format placing every edge its kind declares passes; one that omits an edge is a finding at the clause author's declared severity.

acceptance: An author can declare the check representation.md names: a format that omits an edge its kind declares renders a contract the prose does not represent. Adding the predicate is the ratified language change; no default contract adopts it unbidden.
notes: 0025 bullet 3. Two bars build must not paper over: (1) the spec puts this clause in no default contract — do not invent an adoption; the predicate exists, the author declares it. (2) The engine cannot inspect an erased `render` hook and never reads a projection back (invariant 7), so the placement fact must reach the lock as an emit-captured declaration row. If that spelling does not hold, leave the entry and raise an open question. scoped at cac023a.

summary: The packaging remainder after the linux-x64+win32-x64 first cut: darwin binaries (Apple notarizing), the plugin/bundle + marketplace.json, and the binary↔npm version lockstep at the v0.1 tag

gate.reason: Human release actions: John's Apple notarizing for the darwin standalone binary + darwin optionalDependency package; the v0.1 launch tag that flips the binary's self-reported crate version (0.1.0) into lockstep with npm's 0.0.x. Channel 3 (plugin/bundle + marketplace.json) is a git repo + marketplace.json + free community web form (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs) — no signing creds — but rides the same release-workflow build-out. release.yml (channel 2, linux-x64+win32-x64) + the SDK launcher/optionalDependencies + standalone tag assets shipped 07-11; the standalone-asset step is wired but unexercised until a tag fires.

edit.description: Extend the existing release.yml (linux-x64 + win32-x64 first cut, the darwin deferral stated at line 7) with the darwin matrix targets (aarch64/x86_64-apple-darwin) + an Apple notarization step, and a channel-3 step producing/publishing the `temper bundle` output with its marketplace.json. Distinct from the standalone tag-asset step, already wired (unexercised until a tag).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper on all three OSes without a Rust toolchain (darwin binary notarized + pinned), or via the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); the binary self-reports 0.1.0 in lockstep with the v0.1 npm cut (distribution.md: prebuilt binaries install on all three OSes; lockstep engine pin).
notes: Supersedes retired PACKAGING-CHANNELS (John, 07-11): both its file claims were dead — release.yml now exists (its `new` claim fails the refs gate) and the root-package.json launcher was ruled out (the launcher + optionalDependencies live on the SDK, distribution.md 'What ships'). Release infra outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish. Park re-tested at cc5a9b3: no version tag, crate 0.1.0 vs npm 0.0.7, release.yml still defers darwin. scoped at b676656.
